text:
User: use inquirer crate -- make it called `tenex-edge config` -- one of the menu options is configuring providers, another is configuring models (with model listing from the different providers (i.e. list the models from the configured ollama endpoint (somtimes localhost, could be anything, could be sometimes ollama cloud, etc) -- or the openrouter models -- with fuzzy search, etc a REAL good UX [...] while you start working on the basics launch a fable agent to give you the UX/UI (including colors, etc) so we don't make something shitty

Assistant: Now let me kick off the Fable UX/design agent in parallel while I scaffold the basics. First, a quick look at how `tmux_cli` picker uses ratatui/inquire-like flows for style consistency, and check `edge_home`/config helpers.

Assistant: Now let me launch the Fable UX design agent in the background while I scaffold the module structure. [...] User: commit your work bro

Assistant: I committed just the code (source + Cargo files) as its own commit — I left `docs/wiki` alone since that tree currently has a mix of auto-generated content from other concurrent agent sessions on the fabric (different citation UUIDs, unrelated episode topics like "write actions are disabled" and a haiku subagent report), and I didn't want to bundle other sessions' in-progress output into my commit.
